title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
description: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
description: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

title: The “people’s airline” and the enterprise AI gold rush
description: Everyone wants a piece of the enterprise AI pie, and this week, we saw a string of companies making their moves. From Anthropic and OpenAI announcing new joint ventures targeting enterprise AI deployment to SAP dropping $1B on German AI startup Prior Labs, it’s becoming clear that if you’re a startup building enterprise tools, you’re likely an acquisition target. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, hosts Kirsten Korosec, Anthony […]

title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
description: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried about drowning.

title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
description: The new features could be handy for customer service systems, but OpenAI says they have applications that work across a variety of other fields, including education and creator platforms.

title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
description: AI startup Pit is led by the co-founders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
description: The company is expanding its efforts to protect ChatGPT users in cases where conversations may turn to self-harm.

title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
description: Perplexity's Personal Computer brings AI agents to your Mac, and is now open to everyone.

title: Elon Musk’s lawsuit is putting OpenAI’s safety record under the microscope
description: Elon Musk's legal effort to dismantle OpenAI may hinge on how its for-profit subsidiary enhances or detracts from the frontier lab's founding mission of ensuring that humanity benefits from artificial general intelligence.

title: Bumble is getting rid of the swipe, CEO says
description: Based on Whitney Wolfe Herd's past comments about Bumble's new direction, the company is expected to lean into AI -- Bumble is even working on an AI dating assistant called Bee, and the CEO has made many comments over the years about how AI will be "a supercharger to love and relationships."

title: How Anthropic’s Mythos has rewritten Firefox’s approach to cybersecurity
description: Security researchers at Mozilla say Anthropic's Mythos has unearthed a wealth of high-severity bugs in Firefox.

title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27: A shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
description: Startup Battlefield 200 applications are open, but only for three more weeks. Apply by May 27 for your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, $100,000 equity-free, and more opportunities for major scaling impact.

title: Exhibit at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Get in front of 10,000 decision-makers before space runs out
description: If startup visibility, traction, and real deals matter, you should be on the TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 exhibit floor. Get your 6' exhibit table before your competitor does.

title: Aurora’s Chris Urmson on why self-driving trucks are finally ready to scale
description: Self-driving has been “almost here” for over a decade. But somewhere between DARPA challenges and a handful of driverless trucks hauling freight between Dallas and Houston, Aurora co-founder and CEO Chris Urmson’s story changed. The self-driving truck company started commercial driverless operations last April and is now scaling from a handful of trucks to hundreds this year. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we’re bringing you a […]

title: 2 days left: Get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
description: Two days left to save up to $410 on your pass, and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Offer ends May 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Register now.

title: China’s Moonshot AI raises $2B at $20B valuation as demand for open source AI skyrockets
description: Moonshot's annualized recurring revenue topped $200 million in April, driven by rapid growth in paid subscriptions and API usage.

title: Spotify wants to become the home for AI-generated personal audio
description: Users will be able to create a podcast from Codex or Claude Code and import it to Spotify.

title: Spotify’s AI DJ now supports French, German, Italian, and Brazilian Portuguese
description: Spotify's AI DJ feature now supports French, German, Italian, and Brazilian Portuguese.
